label: Gulf Regional Conflict
subtitle: Indian nationals in UAE / Saudi Arabia / Kuwait / Qatar / Oman / Bahrain
disclaimer: Informational resource only. Compiled from MEA advisories and verified sources. NOT official government guidance. Verify all information at mea.gov.in before acting. Situation changes rapidly.

levels.label: GREEN
levels.name: Normal
levels.description: No active advisory. Normal life. Do prep now - not during a crisis.
levels.officialTrigger: No MEA advisory. Flights operating normally. No travel warning above 'exercise normal precautions.'
levels.observableTrigger: Regional news tense but no conflict in your geography. Employer not discussing contingency. Shops, banks, government services operating normally.

levels.label: YELLOW
levels.name: Watch
levels.description: Geopolitical tension elevated. No direct threat yet. Weeks available. Most people do nothing here and regret it.
levels.officialTrigger: MEA issues 'exercise caution' advisory. Embassy issues community notice. Any elevated travel warning for the region.
levels.observableTrigger: Any 2 of: Indian carrier reduces Gulf routes / Colleagues discussing contingency plans / Local govt issues preparedness communication / Mainstream news mentions military posturing / Insurance or bank asking about contingencies.

nodes.label:
Complete GREEN
prep first
checklist: Return to GREEN level and complete all steps before continuing
MADAD registration is the most critical single action - do it now

nodes.label:
Renew urgently
do not wait
checklist: Passport renewal during a crisis takes weeks you will not have
Visit embassy directly and request urgent processing
Ask employer for time off - this is a safety matter, not optional

nodes.label: Pack go-bag now
checklist: Passport and all document copies
2 weeks of essential medication - get extra prescription now, pharmacies close
Phone, charger, power bank - keep charged at all times from now
Local cash: 2 weeks of living expenses
USD 200-300 minimum for border crossings
One set of clothes
Written contact list on paper: embassy, family, MADAD, bank - phones die

nodes.label:
Withdraw cash
ATMs queue later
checklist: Withdraw 2 weeks of expenses in local currency today
Get USD 200-300 from exchange - do not leave this for later
Check your bank's international transfer limits during emergencies
Consider transferring large savings to India now if comfortable doing so

nodes.label:
Know your
legal standing
checklist: Does your visa require employer consent to exit? Kafala-system visas sometimes do.
Does your employment contract have a force majeure clause?
Does your employer hold your passport? Illegal in all Gulf states. Request return in writing NOW.
Ask HR directly about the company's emergency plan. If they don't have one, that is information.

nodes.label:
Survey flight
options (don't book yet)
checklist: Check Air India: airindia.com
Check IndiGo: indigo.in
Note which routes are still operating and which have been suspended
Note indirect routing options: Istanbul, Colombo, Amman, Cairo all viable
Screenshot current prices - if still low, consider booking a refundable fare

nodes.label:
Activate daily
communication
checklist: Move from weekly to daily check-in
Confirm primary contact in India has: your address, employer, local number, embassy number
Confirm they follow @MEAIndia on X/Twitter for official advisories
Single coordination person only - not everyone calling the embassy simultaneously

levels.label: ORANGE
levels.name: Advisory
levels.description: Active conflict in region. Flights disrupting. Evacuation recommended. Hours to days available. Deliberation is over.
levels.officialTrigger: MEA recommends Indians consider departing or register urgently. Embassy activates repatriation flight registration. DGCA issues Gulf route directives.
levels.observableTrigger: Any 2 of: Multiple airlines suspend routes / Military activity visible or audible / Local govt issues curfew or shelter-in-place / Employer sends staff home / Shops showing shortages or fuel queues.

nodes.label:
SHELTERING
Embassy knows
your location
Escalate to RED
if worsens

Go to RED
protocol now
checklist: You are in immediate danger - open RED level now
Shelter in place immediately
Call embassy 24x7 helpline and state your location

nodes.label:
Call embassy
NOW then book
checklist: Not for general questions - to confirm registration and ask about chartered repatriation flights
Ask specifically: am I on the waitlist? When is the next chartered flight?

nodes.label:
Book earliest
available seat
checklist: Book now - even indirect routing via Istanbul, Colombo, Amman, Cairo
Do not wait for a direct route. Indirect is fine. Waiting is not.
Notify employer in writing that you are departing - WhatsApp to manager is sufficient
Withdraw any remaining cash NOW before ATM queues form
Transfer bank balance to India if possible
Charge all devices to 100%
Screenshot everything offline: contacts, booking, embassy numbers

nodes.label:
Identify overland
route by country
checklist: KUWAIT: Al-Nuwaisib/Salwa crossing to Saudi. KSA visa required: visa.visitsaudi.com. Embassy buses may operate.
UAE: Buraimi or Hatta crossing to Oman. UAE airports also more resilient - verify first.
QATAR: Salwa border to Saudi. Peninsula - no other land exit. KSA visa required.
BAHRAIN: King Fahd Causeway to Saudi. ~25km to Khobar/Dammam.
SAUDI: Riyadh and Jeddah airports most stable. Proceed there.
OMAN: Muscat airport generally operational. Direct flights to India often available.
Fill fuel tank immediately - queues form fast. Take go-bag only, leave large luggage.
Expect border wait times of several hours. Stock water and food for the vehicle.

nodes.label:
Shelter in place
and wait
checklist: Stock 72 hours: water, food, essential medication
Interior room, away from all windows
Keep all devices charged
Contact embassy to register as awaiting evacuation - give them your exact location
Check in with family every 6 hours minimum
Monitor ONLY: embassy official website and @MEAIndia on X/Twitter
Do NOT act on WhatsApp forwards - this is where most harm comes from

levels.label: RED
levels.name: Warning
levels.description: Direct threat. Active strikes nearby. Airspace closing. Immediate danger possible.
levels.officialTrigger: Embassy issues depart immediately or shelter-in-place directive. Airspace closed in your country. MEA activates emergency evacuation.
levels.observableTrigger: You can hear explosions or sirens. Streets emptying. ATMs being drained. Your building or area is targeted.

nodes.label:
Active threat
in your
immediate area?

Family in India
knows you
are in RED?

EXITING
Low and fast
Notify embassy
of your route

HOLDING
Embassy has
your location
Await official
evacuation

SHELTER NOW
immediately
checklist: Move to interior room immediately
Away from all windows - glass is the primary injury risk
Ground floor or basement preferred
Do NOT go outside during an active alert for any reason
Stay until all-clear from official source only - not WhatsApp

nodes.label:
Emergency
documents
checklist: Embassy issues Emergency Certificate of Identity if passport is lost or confiscated
Call embassy 24x7 line - you do not need a passport to contact them
If passport was confiscated by employer: state this explicitly - embassies have specific protocols

nodes.label:
Notify family
and embassy
checklist: One message to primary family contact: I am safe, I am at [location]
Call embassy 24x7 helpline - state you are in RED, give your exact address
Update MADAD status if possible: madad.gov.in
Then conserve phone battery - do not scroll news feeds

nodes.label:
Special cases
protocol
checklist: MEDICAL: local emergency first (UAE: 998 / Saudi: 911 / Kuwait: 112 / Qatar: 999 / Oman: 9999 / Bahrain: 999), then embassy
UNACCOMPANIED MINOR: state this immediately when calling embassy. Family in India: call MEA Control Room +91-11-23011954 now.
PASSPORT CONFISCATED: illegal in all Gulf states. Embassy issues Emergency ID. Do NOT confront employer alone.
DOMESTIC OR LABOUR WORKER: contact embassy welfare cell specifically - handles confiscated passports and employer disputes

nodes.label:
Exit options
by country
checklist: KUWAIT: Al-Nuwaisib/Salwa to Saudi. Embassy buses may operate. community.kuwait@mea.gov.in
UAE: Buraimi or Hatta to Oman. UAE airports also more resilient.
QATAR: Salwa border only land option. cons.doha@mea.gov.in
BAHRAIN: King Fahd Causeway to Khobar.
SAUDI: Riyadh and Jeddah airports most stable. cw.riyadh@mea.gov.in
OMAN: Muscat airport generally operational. cw.muscat@mea.gov.in

nodes.label:
Shelter and
escalate
checklist: Stock 72 hours of water, food, medication - ration if necessary
Keep devices charged - designate one phone for calls only
Call embassy every 12 hours if no update received
Monitor ONLY: embassy official website and @MEAIndia
Do NOT move based on WhatsApp information
Do NOT pay unofficial evacuation agents - use embassy channels only

levels.label: BLACK
levels.name: Emergency
levels.description: Full airspace closure. Direct strikes on civilian infrastructure. Immediate life threat. Embassy may have suspended routine services.
levels.officialTrigger: Full airspace closure across multiple Gulf states. Embassy suspends routine services. Government-to-government evacuation only.
levels.observableTrigger: Explosions audible or visible from your location. Utilities disrupted. Embassy unreachable on normal lines. All commercial exit routes closed.

nodes.label:
HOLD POSITION
Embassy has
your location
Await official
evacuation only

Medical
emergency first
checklist: UAE: 998 / Saudi Arabia: 911 / Kuwait: 112
Qatar: 999 / Oman: 9999 / Bahrain: 999
Do not move an injured person unless there is immediate further danger at the current location

nodes.label:
Move to safer
structure
checklist: Nearest concrete or reinforced building
Embassy or consulate compound if reachable and safe
Hotel with basement or ground-floor interior rooms
Do NOT cross open ground during an active alert
Move low, move fast, move once - do not go back for belongings

nodes.label:
Emergency
embassy contact
checklist: Saudi Arabia: 800 247 1234 / WhatsApp +966-542126748
MEA Control Room from India: +91-11-23011954
WhatsApp often works when voice calls do not. SMS often works when data does not.

nodes.label:
Emergency
supply protocol
checklist: Ration water: 2 litres minimum per person per day
Do not use gas appliances if there is structural damage nearby
All phones on low power mode except one designated for calls
If medication running low: state this when contacting embassy - medical supply is priority in evacuations
Keep everyone in the same room - do not split up
